source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
As shown in the figure, a sufficiently long plank $AB$ with mass $M=2\mathrm{kg}$ moves in a straight line to the right on a horizontal surface. The upper surface of the plank is smooth and is at a height $h=0.2\mathrm{m}$ above the ground. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the lower surface of the plank and the ground is $\mu=0.1$. At a certain moment when the plank's velocity is $v_{0}=9\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}$, a constant horizontal force $F=5\mathrm{N}$ is applied to the left end $A$ of the plank, directed to the left. Simultaneously, a small block with mass $m=2\mathrm{kg}$ is gently placed at the right end $B$ of the plank. After some time, the small block detaches from the plank. The small block can be treated as a point mass, and $g=10\mathbf{m}/\mathbf{s}$.

1. The time it takes for the small wooden block to fall from the board to the ground.
2. The velocity of the wooden board at the moment the small wooden block hits the ground.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'As shown in the figure'
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows 1 valid image and registry_passed=true
the text itself already specifies most numerical values and directions (M, m, h, mu, F, v0, left/right), so the figure may support layout more than provide unique missing data
multi_step: the problem asks for two outputs at different event times: block falling time and plank velocity when the block hits the ground
solving requires combining multiple physics relations: horizontal dynamics of the plank, detachment timing/event sequencing, and vertical fall from height h
the setup includes several interacting conditions and parameters (force, friction, smooth top surface, placement at end B, later detachment)
verifiability: answer_text contains two concrete numeric results, which are in principle checkable against physics derivation or numeric simulation
the question provides explicit constants and quantities (including g=10), which supports deterministic checking
the final answer format is brief and unlabeled across two lines, so verification likely needs tolerance and output-order assumptions

decision_reasons: Question text, answer, and the referenced image are all present, and the asset registry passed with no recorded issues.
Initial scores are strong for multi-step reasoning and verifiability, indicating good candidate value.
The image dependency is meaningful but supported by an available valid image, so core semantics appear intact.
The noted risks are relatively minor: the figure may be supporting rather than strictly necessary, and the two numeric answers are unlabeled but still recoverable.

open_ended_problem_variants.title: PhysReason 开放题
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text:
As shown in the figure, a sufficiently long plank $AB$ with mass $M=2\mathrm{kg}$ moves in a straight line to the right on a horizontal surface. The upper surface of the plank is smooth and is at a height $h=0.2\mathrm{m}$ above the ground. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the lower surface of the plank and the ground is $\mu=0.1$. At a certain moment when the plank's velocity is $v_{0}=9\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}$, a constant horizontal force $F=5\mathrm{N}$ is applied to the left end $A$ of the plank, directed to the left. Simultaneously, a small block with mass $m=2\mathrm{kg}$ is gently placed at the right end $B$ of the plank. After some time, the small block detaches from the plank. The small block can be treated as a point mass, and $g=10\mathbf{m}/\mathbf{s}$.

1. The time it takes for the small wooden block to fall from the board to the ground.
2. The velocity of the wooden board at the moment the small wooden block hits the ground.
